hooks.name: update-docs-on-api-change
hooks.description: Automatically synchronize API documentation when route handlers or API contracts change. Reads the service spec and updates docs/api/README.md and OpenAPI specs to reflect new endpoints, changed signatures, or modified behaviors. Eliminates manual doc updates.

action.prompt:
You are updating API documentation after route handler changes.

CONTEXT:
- The API route handler or controller has changed: ${KIRO_CHANGED_FILES}
- Service spec location: ${SPEC_DIR:-specs}/
- API documentation: ${API_DOCS_PATH:-docs/api/README.md}
- OpenAPI spec: ${OPENAPI_SPEC_PATH:-docs/openapi.yaml}

YOUR TASK:
1. Read the service spec for this project to understand the intended API contracts
2. Analyze the changed route handler to identify:
   - New endpoints (if any)
   - Modified endpoint signatures (path, method, parameters)
   - Changed request/response schemas
   - Updated error codes or status codes
   - New or modified authentication requirements

3. Update ${API_DOCS_PATH:-docs/api/README.md} with:
   - New endpoint documentation (if endpoint was added)
   - Updated endpoint signatures (if existing endpoint changed)
   - Current request/response examples
   - Error handling documentation
   - Authentication/authorization requirements
   - Rate limiting information (if applicable)

4. If ${OPENAPI_SPEC_PATH:-docs/openapi.yaml} exists, update it with:
   - New path definitions
   - Updated schema definitions
   - Revised parameter specifications
   - Current response codes and schemas

5. Maintain consistency:
   - Use the same format as existing documentation
   - Keep alphabetical ordering of endpoints (if that's the current style)
   - Match example style (curl, HTTP, code snippets)
   - Preserve any custom notes or warnings

DOCUMENTATION STYLE:
- Format: ${DOC_FORMAT:-markdown}
- Detail level: ${MARKDOWN_STYLE:-simple}
- Include examples: ${GENERATE_EXAMPLES:-true}
- Include deprecated endpoints: ${INCLUDE_DEPRECATED:-false}

IMPORTANT:
- Do NOT remove existing endpoints unless explicitly deleted in code
- Do NOT change the overall documentation structure
- Do NOT modify unrelated sections
- If uncertain about endpoint behavior, reference the spec
- If spec is outdated or missing details, flag this in the doc update

VERIFICATION:
After updating documentation:
- Verify all endpoint paths match the code
- Verify request/response schemas are accurate
- Verify authentication requirements are current
- Check that examples are valid and up-to-date

metadata.purpose: Automatically update API documentation when route handlers or API contracts change. Keeps docs/api/README.md and OpenAPI specs in sync with code without manual updates.
requirements: 5.1: Auto-update API documentation when route handlers change
5.5: Inline customization guidance for doc structure

required: Update API_DOCS_PATH to point to your API documentation directory (default: docs/api/README.md)
Update OPENAPI_SPEC_PATH if you maintain OpenAPI/Swagger specs (default: docs/openapi.yaml)
Adjust matcher regex to match your API route file patterns

workflow_integration: scaffold-service.json - Generates initial docs when new spec is created
validate-spec-constraints.json - Ensures code matches spec before doc generation
metadata.time_savings: Manual documentation: 15-30 minutes per endpoint change. Automated with this hook: <1 minute. Average API with 20 endpoints: ~10 hours/month saved.
best_practices: Keep your service spec up to date - it's the source of truth
Review auto-generated docs periodically for accuracy
Add custom notes/warnings manually in dedicated sections
Combine with spec-first workflow for best results

troubleshooting.docs_not_updating: Check matcher regex matches your file structure
troubleshooting.wrong_doc_format: Adjust DOC_FORMAT and MARKDOWN_STYLE environment variables
troubleshooting.missing_spec_file: Check SPEC_DIR points to your spec directory

compliance: API documentation is often required for SOC 2, ISO 27001
Auto-sync ensures documentation is current for audits
Maintains audit trail of when docs were updated (git history)
Reduces risk of undocumented API changes
